sections.title: 1. "N+1"-shaped: shared state causes every book card to redo work it doesn't need
2. Unnecessary re-renders
sections.content:
**`AudioPlayer.tsx:69`** ‚Äî `voicesForUi()` (filter + `.sort()` with string ops in `voiceScore`) runs inline in JSX on every render of `AudioPlayer`. `AudioPlayer` re-renders on every `useTts()` state change, which fires on every TTS sentence-boundary event (multiple times per second during playback) since `emit()` (`tts.ts:47-50`) always creates a new state object and `useSyncExternalStore` re-renders all subscribers. That's a sort + string manipulation over the whole voice list running many times a second for no reason ‚Äî the voice list only actually changes when `voicesVersion` bumps.

```tsx
// AudioPlayer.tsx
const voiceList = useMemo(() => voicesForUi(), [s.voicesVersion])
...
{voiceList.map((v) => ...)}
```

**`Reader.tsx:100-109`** ‚Äî the scroll handler calls `setPct` on every raw `scroll` event with no throttling:
```ts
window.addEventListener('scroll', onScroll, { passive: true })
```
On a long chapter this fires dozens of times per second, each one a state update and re-render of the whole `Reader` tree. Throttle via `requestAnimationFrame`:
```ts
let ticking = false
const onScroll = () => {
  if (ticking) return
  ticking = true
  requestAnimationFrame(() => {
    const el = document.documentElement
    const max = el.scrollHeight - window.innerHeight
    setPct(max > 0 ? Math.min(100, (window.scrollY / max) * 100) : 100)
    ticking = false
  })
}
```

**`BookCard` / `MapChapterCard`** (`Library.tsx:29`, `BookMap.tsx:22`) aren't wrapped in `React.memo`, so any parent re-render (search keystroke, progress change) re-renders every card even when its own props are unchanged.

sections.title: 3. Caching opportunities
4. Memory leaks
sections.content:
Nothing acute ‚Äî cleanup is handled carefully throughout (`ttsStop()` on unmount in `BookMap.tsx:81`/`Reader.tsx:92`, listener/interval teardown in `tts.ts`'s `stopKeepalive`, highlight/class cleanup in `follow.ts:100-106`). Two things worth a second look, not urgent:

- **`tts.ts:70-73`**: `speechSynthesis.addEventListener('voiceschanged', refreshVoices)` is registered once at module scope and never removed. Harmless since it's a true app-lifetime singleton, but if this module were ever hot-reloaded repeatedly in dev, listeners would accumulate. Not worth fixing for a page-lifetime singleton.
- **`follow.ts:96-108` `rangeForNormalizedOffsets`** walks the entire element's DOM with a fresh `TreeWalker` on every sentence-boundary event rather than resuming from the last position. Not a leak, but on a very long chapter body this is redundant work fired multiple times per second during playback ‚Äî could track `pos`/node across calls if it ever shows up as a bottleneck.

sections.title: 5. Redundant computations
